home.description: Talmud Eser Sefirot, read as aligned summary, source, and commentary — in English and Hebrew.
home.heroSubtitle: The Study of the Ten Sefirot
home.quoteSource: Talmud Eser Sefirot, Section I, Chapter 1
home.howItWorksSourceBody: The numbered paragraphs of the text itself, read straight through, chapter by chapter.
home.howItWorksLightBody: Line-by-line commentary anchored to individual paragraphs, opening each phrase in turn.
home.howItWorksObservationBody: A reflective essay on each chapter's ideas, read as a continuous layer of its own.
home.coverageHebrew: Hebrew is complete for Part I — all 119 sections, from the 1956 Jerusalem edition.
home.coverageEnglish: English is partial: two chapters have a full translation, and all ten Inner Observation chapters have AI-assisted translations, clearly marked as such.
home.coverageMore: The remaining volumes are being added over time.

volumes.indexDescription: Talmud Eser Sefirot in six volumes, sixteen parts — read as aligned summary, source, and commentary.
volumes.partComingSoon: This part hasn't been added yet — check back soon.

about.title: About Read TES
about.intro: Read TES is an independent, interactive reading of Talmud Eser Sefirot — Baruch Ashlag's foundational commentary on the Kabbalah of the Ari — built by students of Bnei Baruch Kabbalah Academy for anyone studying the text closely, in Hebrew or in English.
about.readingModelBody: Each chapter is read item by item, the way it is studied in a live lesson: a numbered paragraph of the source text, opened by its Inner Light commentary where one exists, and set beside the chapter's Inner Observation essay as a further layer of reflection. Nothing is paraphrased away — every layer links back to the same paragraph number.
about.licensesIntro: Every text edition on this site is listed below with its language, license, and source.
about.licensesTableCaption: Text editions used in Read TES
about.attributionBody: Source texts are drawn from Sefaria's open library, and the lessons this reading is inspired by are from KabbalahMedia.info — both used with permission.
